title: Business Risk Heatmap and Early Warning System
dataSources: Threshold and banding metadata: lookup file `business_risk_thresholds.csv` (columns `risk_area`, `risk_metric`, `unit`, `green_threshold`, `amber_threshold`, `direction`, optional `threshold_version`, `effective_from`) stored under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/<your_app>/lookups/` with a matching `transforms.conf` stanza. Financial and HR facts: `index=business` with Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) sourcetypes such as `dbx:erp_orders` (revenue), `dbx:ar_invoices` (open receivables and due dates), `dbx:hris_employees` (terminations, headcount keys). Customer voice: `index=business` `sourcetype=nps_survey` (or coalesce from Medallia/Qualtrics-style feeds). Cyber signal: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) — `` `notable` `` macro (notable index), urgency and severity fields on notable events. Service operations: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:incident` (ServiceNow Splunk Add-on patterns) for SLA breach math. Platform health overlay: `index=itsi_summary` KPI aggregates from Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841) — use `` `get_full_kpi_summary` `` or scheduled writes to summary when wiring executive tiles; optional `itsi_grouped_alerts` / `itsi_tracked_alerts` for narrative links. Every index name above must appear in role `srchIndexesAllowed` for the run-as identity used by scheduled executive searches.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security ([Splunkbase 263](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)) for the `` `notable` `` macro and Incident Review workflows; Splunk IT Service Intelligence ([Splunkbase 1841](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)) for KPI summary index material and optional service-level health overlays; Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) for JDBC pulls from ERP, AR, and HRIS. Splunk ITSI and Splunk Enterprise Security are premium products — production Glass Table, KPI objects, and Enterprise Security Notable workflows require separate entitlements beyond core Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud platform capacity.
spl:
| inputlookup business_risk_thresholds.csv
| append [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:erp_orders" earliest=-30d
    | stats sum(revenue) as value
    | eval risk_area="Financial", risk_metric="Monthly Revenue", unit="currency"]
| append [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:ar_invoices" status="open" earliest=-1d
    | eval days_overdue=round((now()-strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
    | stats avg(days_overdue) as value
    | eval risk_area="Financial", risk_metric="DSO", unit="days"]
| append [
    search index=business sourcetype="nps_survey" earliest=-30d
    | stats avg(score) as nps_avg
    | eval value=round(nps_avg,1), risk_area="Customer", risk_metric="NPS Score", unit="points"]
| append [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:hris_employees" earliest=-90d
    | eval termed=if(isnotnull(termination_date) AND strptime(termination_date,"%Y-%m-%d") > relative_time(now(),"-90d"),1,0)
    | stats sum(termed) as terms_90d, dc(employee_id) as headcount
    | eval value=round(100*(terms_90d/headcount)*4,1)
    | eval risk_area="People", risk_metric="Annualised Attrition %", unit="pct"]
| append [
    search `notable` urgency IN ("critical","high") earliest=-30d
    | stats count as value
    | eval risk_area="Cyber", risk_metric="Critical ES Notables", unit="count"]
| append [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" sla_breached="true" earliest=-30d
    | stats count as breached, count(eval(state!="Closed")) as total
    | eval value=round(100*breached/total,1)
    | eval risk_area="Operational", risk_metric="SLA Breach Rate", unit="pct"]
| where isnotnull(value)
| lookup business_risk_thresholds.csv risk_metric OUTPUT green_threshold, amber_threshold, direction
| eval risk_level=case(
    direction="higher_is_better" AND value >= green_threshold, "GREEN",
    direction="higher_is_better" AND value >= amber_threshold, "AMBER",
    direction="higher_is_better", "RED",
    direction="lower_is_better" AND value <= green_threshold, "GREEN",
    direction="lower_is_better" AND value <= amber_threshold, "AMBER",
    direction="lower_is_better", "RED",
    1=1, "UNKNOWN")
| eval delta_to_amber=case(
    direction="higher_is_better", round(value - amber_threshold, 1),
    direction="lower_is_better", round(amber_threshold - value, 1))
| sort risk_area, risk_metric
| table risk_area, risk_metric, value, unit, green_threshold, amber_threshold, risk_level, delta_to_amber
description: Consolidates financial, customer, people, cyber, and operational indicators into one threshold-scored table by chaining DB Connect–sourced business facts, Splunk Enterprise Security notable counts, and (when extended) ITSI summary rows, then joining each computed `risk_metric` to `business_risk_thresholds.csv` for directional green, amber, and red bands. The pattern is explicitly cross-domain: it is not a single-domain finance scorecard (UC-23.8.1), a cash-cycle efficiency construct (UC-23.8.6), or a productivity ratio pack (UC-23.8.2); it exists so Audit Committee and C-suite reviews see one harmonised risk surface aligned to COSO ERM-style tolerance statements rather than five siloed slide decks.
value: Board risk registers and weekly executive huddles need a reconciled narrative—when revenue softness, rising DSO, NPS slips, HR exits, SLA stress, and surging critical notables move together, waiting for separate PDFs invites blind spots and late capital or staffing decisions. Splunk becomes the aggregation plane: DB Connect lands authoritative numbers, ES supplies security-notable intensity, ITSI can mirror customer-facing or fulfilment KPIs from `itsi_summary`, and the lookup encodes the risk appetite the board signed. That combination shortens time-to-decision on mitigations, gives Internal Audit traceable thresholds (with version stamps), and feeds Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 and ISO 31000-style evidence that monitoring exists—without asking executives to interpret raw SPL or raw SIEM queues.
implementation: (1) Publish `business_risk_thresholds.csv` under change control with `direction` per metric (`higher_is_better` vs `lower_is_better`), optional `threshold_version`, and rows that match `risk_metric` strings in SPL exactly (including spacing). (2) Install and license Splunk Enterprise Security (263) and Splunk ITSI (1841) on search heads that will run executive content; deploy DB Connect (2686) on a supported execution node with read-only JDBC to ERP/HRIS. (3) Ingest `index=business` sourcetypes (`dbx:erp_orders`, `dbx:ar_invoices`, `nps_survey`, `dbx:hris_employees`), route ServiceNow incidents to `index=itsm` `snow:incident`, and confirm the `notable` macro resolves for risk run-as roles. (4) Schedule the saved search after the slowest authoritative batch (often DB Connect night jobs) and snapshot results to a summary index or KV artifact for dashboard single-value tiles. (5) Attach executive notification when any `risk_level` transitions to RED or when `threshold_version` changes mid-period.
visualization: Risk heatmap panel (risk_area × banded colour from `risk_level`). Single-value tiles per risk_area with twelve-point sparkline of `value` pulled from an `outputlookup`-backed history or summary index. Risk-register table showing `risk_metric`, `risk_level`, `delta_to_amber`, `threshold_version`, last refresh time, named owner from a `risk_register_owners.csv` join, and disposition for Audit Committee quarterly packs. Trend chart of `risk_level` encodings month-over-month (map GREEN/AMBER/RED to 1/2/3 for readability). Correlation scatter or dual-axis chart: Financial `Monthly Revenue` series from the same summary index versus Cyber `Critical ES Notables` count to illustrate stress coupling for board narrative (not causation). Optional ITSI Glass Table lane for services whose KPIs feed the same executive story.

references.title: COSO — Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) Integrating with Strategy and Performance (2017)

knownFalsePositives: Band colours can flip when the lookup tightens (for example revenue amber floor from nine million to nine point five million) even though operations did not change—always pair heatmap tiles with `threshold_version` and `effective_from` in the footnote. Splunk Security Content deployments can temporarily raise critical and high notable counts because new detections increase coverage, not because adversary activity spiked—compare to Enterprise Security Incident Review disposition rates and escape-hatch exclusions before declaring cyber regression. Quarter-end revenue recognition pulls can swing Monthly Revenue from amber to green the final week then back without a structural fix. NPS based on fewer than thirty responses in the window is statistically thin; guard with `where response_count>=30` in a sibling panel. M&A headcount integration lags make Annualised Attrition rate look artificially low until acquired employees appear in the HRIS feed. Multi-currency ERP roll-ups that translate at month-end spot versus daily snapshots inject two-to-five percent noise into Financial rows—Finance must own the FX policy referenced in the risk register. ServiceNow SLA logic changes (pause conditions) alter SLA breach rate without a process change on the shop floor.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: KPI summary index, service health, optional Glass Table executive lane

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise 9.2+ or Splunk Cloud; separate license entitlements for Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) and Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841) on the search heads that materialise executive dashboards.
• Splunk DB Connect (splunk_app_db_connect, Splunkbase 2686) with JDBC connectivity and documented query schedules; read-only database roles for revenue, AR open items, and HRIS termination fields.
• Knowledge objects: `business_risk_thresholds.csv` in `lookups/` with matching `transforms.conf` definition; optional `risk_register_owners.csv` keyed by `risk_metric` for Audit Committee owner columns.
• Role permissions covering `index=business`, `index=notable`, `index=itsm`, `index=itsi_summary`, and the summary index you choose for historised tiles; Enterprise Security content requires powers to run the `` `notable` `` macro without exposing unrelated security data—often a dedicated executive-risk role and constrained post-process saved searches.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Path A — Financial and HR via DB Connect: create Data Inputs that emit `dbx:erp_orders` with `revenue`, `transaction_date`, and company keys; `dbx:ar_invoices` with `due_date`, `status`, and open-item flags; `dbx:hris_employees` with `termination_date` and stable `employee_id`. Stamp `_time` from the business event or extraction clock Finance approves. Path B — Customer NPS: land `nps_survey` through HEC or DB Connect to a survey fact table; retain `score` (0–10 for Bain-style NPS math if you adopt promoters minus detractors elsewhere—here the backbone uses raw `score` averages; align the lookup thresholds to the same convention). Path C — ServiceNow: deploy Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow so incidents index as `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:incident` with `sla_breached`, `state`, and priority fields populated; validate field names against your transform (Incident Management UI lists should reconcile counts). Path D — Enterprise Security: confirm the `` `notable` `` macro resolves to `index=notable` (or ES-specific notable index) on the search head and that urgency values include `critical` and `high`. Path E — ITSI (optional extension): schedule a KPI base search writing one row per monitored business service into `itsi_summary`, or permit ad-hoc searches against `index=itsi_summary` using documented `kpi_name`, `alert_severity`, and `health_score` fields—keep ITSI macros on versions per vendor release notes (`get_full_kpi_summary` requires supported ITSI releases; check your deployed app version before relying on bundled macros).

Step 2 — Build the consolidated search and schedule
Embed the SPL from the `spl` field verbatim for the first production cut; retarget only `index=` and `sourcetype=` strings to the names in your ingestion charter. After each `append` subsearch returns a single aggregate row, the outer `lookup` enriches with `green_threshold`, `amber_threshold`, and `direction`; the subsequent `eval` translates bands into GREEN, AMBER, or RED using directional rules (revenue higher is better; DSO in days lower is better). Add a companion scheduled search that writes the compact result set to `index=summary_risk_heatmap` (name as you prefer) via `collect` or `outputlookup` rotation so dashboards do not re-run seven subsearches on every page load—this mitigates append serialization cost at scale. For ITSI-specific rows, either union an additional `append` block querying `index=itsi_summary` for an agreed `kpi_name` (for example order-to-ship delay) or bind a KPI base search that mirrors the same `risk_metric` label used in the CSV. Keep `risk_metric` strings unique and stable for lookup joins.

Step 3 — Validate against source systems
Reconcile Monthly Revenue to the CFO control total in the ERP revenue workspace or management reporting cube for the same window—tolerance usually ≤0.5 percent when definitions match; compare against the SAP billing UI or Oracle revenue inquiry UI your controller signs off on when those systems are authoritative. Compare DSO proxy from `avg(days_overdue)` to finance’s aged trial balance narrative; if your SQL limits to certain customer classes, document the filter beside the tile. Spot-check NPS against the survey vendor’s thirty-day dashboard; if the vendor reports promoter/detractor NPS while Splunk averages raw scores, recalibrate the lookup thresholds rather than forcing the SPL to guess. Validate Annualised Attrition rate against HR’s rolling attrition report for the same ninety-day termination rule. For Critical ES Notables, open Enterprise Security Incident Review and filter urgency for critical and high in the matching window—counts should align within indexing lag. For SLA breach rate, compare denominator logic to the ServiceNow incident list filtered by your breach flag; if `total` is zero because the field is missing, fix extraction before trusting the percentage. Where ITSI is used, open Service Analyzer for the mapped service and compare KPI numeric to the `itsi_summary` row picked up by Splunk Search.

Step 4 — Operationalize cadence and evidence
Weekly C-suite risk stand-up: publish the saved search to a Dashboard Studio board restricted to executive and internal-audit roles; include a footnote listing data-freshness timestamps per domain (ERP batch completion, survey close, HRIS effective date, ES index lag, ServiceNow replication). Monthly: archive a CSV or PDF snapshot to the governance share for Sarbanes-Oxley control testing references. Quarterly Audit Committee: join the heatmap export to `risk_register_owners.csv` with control IDs that map to ISO 31000 and NIST CSF 2.0 narratives your organisation already maintains—this UC supplies the monitoring evidence row, not the entire ERM register. Alerting: trigger email or Teams webhooks when any `risk_level` becomes RED or when `delta_to_amber` crosses zero in the worsening direction; suppress duplicate pages using a state table keyed by `risk_metric` and day.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• Threshold recalibration mid-period — tightening `amber_threshold` from ninety to eighty-five percent can re-colour tiles without operational change; carry `threshold_version` in `business_risk_thresholds.csv` and show it beside every cell.
• Subsearch row-limit truncation — default `subsearch_maxout` (ten thousand) can clip a high-volume `` `notable` `` `append` if your estate generates more than the cap in thirty days; raise `maxout` under `limits.conf` `[subsearch]` for the search-head class that owns this saved search after capacity review, or pre-aggregate Enterprise Security counts hourly into a summary index.
• Index-time versus search-time alignment — DB Connect jobs that land at 02:00 UTC make a risk run at 01:30 UTC appear one day stale; schedule the executive search thirty minutes after the slowest batch and print `latest _indextime` on the dashboard.
• Missing ITSM feed or sourcetype drift — if the ServiceNow feed is absent, the operational `append` returns no rows and the Operational area vanishes silently; add a health search that alerts when `snow:incident` event count falls below policy, and document a temporary Operational placeholder row only if governance permits synthetic placeholders.
• ITSI macro and version coupling — `` `get_full_kpi_summary` `` behaviour differs across ITSI releases; validate macro expansion on a lab search head before embedding in production SPL; if ITSI is not licensed, omit ITSI app objects and rely on raw `index=itsi_summary` searches.
• Enterprise Security content pulses — freshly enabled ESCU modules may inflate notable counts—compare week-over-week against MITRE technique spread and tuning states before escalating cyber risk colour.
• Direction inversion mistakes — assigning `lower_is_better` to Monthly Revenue accidentally paints strong quarters red; audit `direction` quarterly whenever Finance edits targets.
• Thin NPS samples — monthly responses below thirty make averages unstable; gate dashboard publishing with `response_count` from the survey feed.
• Quarter-end revenue pull-forward — recognise calendar artefacts where the final week looks artificially strong and week one of the next quarter looks weak—annotate board slides accordingly.
• Headcount denominator drift after acquisitions — acquired employees often load late into the HRIS; attrition denominators may be understated until the integration cutover completes.
• Currency conversion noise — global revenue rolled at month-end FX versus daily rates shifts Financial colour bands slightly; Finance should own a single translation table referenced in narrative.
• Stale last-known values — without freshness logic, tiles can show yesterday’s green while feeds stopped; compute `data_age_hours` from `_indextime` and grey out cells beyond twenty-four hours.
• Cross-domain coupling — severe cyber RED states may precede Operational RED when incidents flood the ITSM queue; document in the risk narrative that correlation is not causation unless your SOAR playbooks prove linkage.
• Sequential append cost — long `append` chains run subsearches serially; for sub-second dashboards, shift to `multisearch` or move each domain to summary-index collectors written on staggered cron schedules.
• No ServiceNow incidents indexed — when OAuth to the ServiceNow Table API expires, `snow:incident` volume drops to zero while UI lists still show tickets; renew credentials and replay the missed window.
• Permission denied on notable index — executives see blank Cyber rows when the run-as identity lacks `notable` read; grant index rights to the scheduled-search identity only after Information Security reviews the separation-of-duties packet.
grandmaExplanation: We blend money, customer mood, staffing changes, service slip-ups, and serious security warnings into one simple red-yellow-green picture so leaders see where trouble is building before five different departments send five different stories.
